message:
chore(release): bump version to 0.2.2

Increment version from 0.2.1 to 0.2.2 for the patch release containing
installer improvements:

Changes since v0.2.1:
- fix(install): use redirect-based version resolution (c694251)
  Replaces GitHub API calls with redirect-based detection to avoid
  rate limiting issues. The /releases/latest endpoint redirects to
  the actual release URL, from which we extract the version tag.

- docs(readme): move install comment outside code block (184951c)
  Improves README formatting by placing contextual comments outside
  the code fence for better readability.

- fix(install): use fixed lock file path for concurrent install protection (6bfdd6a)
  Fixes bug where LOCK_FILE used shell PID expansion ($) making each
  process get a unique lock file, defeating concurrent install blocking.

Files changed:
- package.json: version 0.2.1 → 0.2.2
- install.sh: fallback version v0.2.1 → v0.2.2

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@ resolve_version() {
     info "Resolved latest version: $VERSION"
   else
     # Fallback to known-good version (update when releasing major versions)
-    VERSION="v0.2.1"
+    VERSION="v0.2.2"
     warn "Could not resolve latest version; defaulting to $VERSION"
   fi
 }

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 {
   "name": "cass-memory",
-  "version": "0.2.1",
+  "version": "0.2.2",
   "type": "module",
   "description": "Universal memory system for AI coding agents - implements ACE framework",
   "author": "Jeffrey Emanuel",
